questions.question: What is the purpose of a method in a JavaScript object?
options: A) To define a variable
B) To create an array
C) To perform an action on an object
D) To declare a function
questions.explanation: Methods are functions that belong to objects and are used to perform specific actions on the object.

questions.question: What is the difference between a property and a method in a JavaScript object?
options: A) Methods are constants, properties are variables
B) Methods are functions, properties are values
C) Methods are used to get data, properties are used to set data
D) Methods are used to set data, properties are used to get data
questions.explanation: Properties are used to store data, while methods are used to perform actions on that data.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the this keyword in a JavaScript method?
options: A) To access a global variable
B) To access an object's own properties and methods
C) To declare a function
D) To return a value
questions.explanation: The this keyword refers to the object the method belongs to, allowing it to access the object's own properties and methods.

questions.question: Can a method in a JavaScript object be static?
options: A) Yes, a method can be static and belong to an object
B) Yes, a method can be static and belong to a class
C) No, methods in objects are always non-static
D) No, methods in objects are always static
questions.explanation: Yes, a method can be static and belong to a class, but not directly to an object.

questions.question: What is the difference between an object literal and an object constructor?
options: A) An object literal is used to create a new object, an object constructor is used to extend an existing object
B) An object literal is used to extend an existing object, an object constructor is used to create a new object
C) An object literal is used to create a new object, an object constructor is used to create a new object
D) An object literal is used to extend an existing object, an object constructor is used to extend an existing object
questions.explanation: An object literal is used to create a new object, while an object constructor is used to extend an existing object.
